name: Patient A
presenting_complaint: Vague jaw discomfort and mild shortness of breath, on and off for the last 2 hours.
history: Hypertension on amlodipine. Long-standing GERD on PPI. Ex-smoker (quit 8 years ago, 20 pack-year). Father died of MI at 64. Denies prior cardiac history.

trajectory.state: Mild jaw discomfort, no acute distress. Vitals as above.
Discomfort persists; HR creeps to 98, BP stable. Mild diaphoresis.
Worsening discomfort radiating to left arm. HR 105, BP 132/84, becoming pale.
Frank chest pain 7/10, diaphoretic, HR 118, BP 118/72. Dynamic ischemic ECG changes if untreated.

red_herrings: Long-standing GERD on PPI — symptoms could plausibly be reflux
